title: Data Analysis Basics
content: Data analysis involves examining, cleaning, transforming, and modeling data to discover useful information, suggest conclusions, and support decision-making.

title: Correlation Analysis
content: Correlation analysis measures the strength and direction of the linear relationship between two variables. Values range from -1 to 1, where 1 indicates perfect positive correlation, -1 indicates perfect negative correlation, and 0 indicates no correlation.

title: Bivariate Analysis
content: Bivariate analysis involves analyzing the relationship between two variables. It can include correlation analysis, scatter plots, and cross-tabulation to understand how variables relate to each other.

title: Dataset Validation
content: Dataset validation involves checking data quality, completeness, and consistency. This includes identifying missing values, outliers, duplicate records, and ensuring data follows expected formats.

title: Machine Learning Models
content: Machine learning models are algorithms that can learn patterns from data to make predictions or classifications. Common types include linear regression, decision trees, random forests, and neural networks.
